content.problemStatement:
You are given an array aaa of length nnn. A subsequence of this array is valid, if it satisfies these two conditions:


There shouldn't be any two even numbers within a distance of KKK, both which have been chosen in the subsequence. i.e. there shouldn't be two indices i,ji, ji,j such that aia_iai​ and aja_jaj​ are even, ∣i−j∣≤K|i - j| \leq K∣i−j∣≤K and aia_iai​ and aja_jaj​ are in the subsequence.
Similarly, there shouldn't be any two odd numbers within a distance of KKK, both which have been chosen in the subsequence.
The sum of a subsequence is the sum of all the numbers in it. Your task is to find the maximum sum possible in a valid subsequence of the given array. Print this maximum sum.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers n,kn, kn,k.
The second line of each test case contains nnn space-separated integers denoting the array aaa.


Output Format
For each test case, output an integer corresponding to the answer of the problem.
constraints: Sum of n over all the test cases doesn't exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers n, k. The second line of each test case contains n space-separated integers denoting the array a.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output an integer corresponding to the answer of the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 2: Only one of the two 2s can be chosen. Hence the answer is 2.
Testcase 3: The subsequence containing the second, third and fifth numbers is a valid subsequence, and its sum is 2+3+6=11. You can check that this is the maximum possible, and hence is the answer.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming. We will maintain two arrays, one for the maximum sum we can get by including the current element and another for the maximum sum we can get by excluding the current element. We will iterate over the array and for each element, we will check if it is even or odd and update the arrays accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n) — where T is the number of test cases and n is the size of the array. This is because we are iterating over the array for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — where n is the size of the array. This is because we are using two arrays of size n to store the maximum sum we can get by including and excluding the current element.
